toolheads.name: Rx
toolheads.description: Toolhead for Voron 2.4 and Trident, based on Bambulab A1 toolhead, features A1/H2 motor, gears, and hotend, CPAP or 5015 cooling.
toolheads.title: Rx

_new_item_sources.Bambu X1/P1: Thank-you, Used a x1c hotend on my Sovol SV08. Read a few hundred degrees off. lol. Sovol uses a 11k pull up value as there standard settings. Had to drop to 5k to read ambient temp equal to my bed, Could not find what i was suppose to enter for the extruder. Hopefully this hotend is not to short, or i will have to raise my bed.
_new_item_sources.EBB36: PG7 Cable Connector (only if using EBB36 v1.2) → Amazon
_new_item_sources.hotend_fan::3010: I changed it to a 3010 fan + shroud, and now the heatsink is only a cool 28 degrees when printing at 250 degrees. But I'm still worried about the backplate melting issue
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::CPAP: The 5015 fan with a well-designed duct can handle almost anything I throw at it, achieving up to 45 mm³/s flow without issues. For even more aggressive cooling, you might want to check out my RX Toolhead CPAP version .
_new_item_sources.part_cooling_fan::5015: The 5015 fan with a well-designed duct can handle almost anything I throw at it, achieving up to 45 mm³/s flow without issues. For even more aggressive cooling, you might want to check out my RX Toolhead CPAP version .
_new_item_sources.filament_cutter: Torsion Spring 1×5×3 – 120° (for filament cutter) → Amazon | Bambu Lab Store
_new_item_sources.printer::SV08: Thank-you, Used a x1c hotend on my Sovol SV08. Read a few hundred degrees off. lol. Sovol uses a 11k pull up value as there standard settings. Had to drop to 5k to read ambient temp equal to my bed, Could not find what i was suppose to enter for the extruder. Hopefully this hotend is not to short, or i will have to raise my bed.
